meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between 'student' and 'engineer' by mastering analytics. A high-impact guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to prove your app's value.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' trap, you must prove your code delivers real-world impact. By integrating robust analytics into your student projects, you transform static repositories into data-backed case studies. This guide provides the exact technical workflow to track user behavior, demonstrating professional-grade engineering readiness to potential employers.
content.intro:
The screen is dark, the repo is empty, and the job market feels like a fortress with no gate. You have the skills, but without a portfolio that speaks in the language of business metrics, you are just another resume in a sea of noise. The difference between a student project and a production-ready application isn't just the code; it is the visibility into how that code performs in the wild.

Today, we stop building in the dark. We are going to wire your application with enterprise-grade analytics, transforming your project from a 'homework assignment' into a measurable success story. With Antigravity’s architecture and Claude Code’s automation, you will stop guessing if your app works and start showing exactly how many users love it.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the specific user action that proves your app's value. This turns a generic project into a business case.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project architecture and suggest a tracking strategy for a North Star Metric that demonstrates user retention and feature engagement.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Identify key user events based on the current codebase logic'

steps.title: Automated Event Instrumentation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to inject event listeners into your core components without polluting your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, decoupled analytics wrapper that handles event batching and error recovery for my current tech stack.

steps.title: Data Visualization Layer
steps.description: Configure your analytics dashboard to pull real-time data, ensuring you have a visual proof-of-concept for your portfolio site.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SQL schema and dashboard layout configuration that visualizes user flow from initial landing to final task completion.

steps.title: Automating Performance Monitoring
steps.description: Integrate latency tracking to show employers you care about user experience and performance optimization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a performance interceptor that logs API response times and client-side rendering delays to my analytics store.

steps.title: Portfolio Integration Hook
steps.description: Expose your analytics results via a public-facing API endpoint or a summary card for your portfolio README.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure, read-only analytics snapshot generator that summarizes monthly active users for a public portfolio display.

pro_tips: Always track 'time-to-first-value' (TTFV) to prove your app solves problems quickly.
Never log sensitive PII; sanitize all event payloads using a middleware filter.
Use cohort analysis to show how your latest code updates improved user retention.
Tag your analytics events with specific versions to demonstrate your deployment lifecycle.
Set up automated alerts for high-latency events to show you manage technical debt proactively.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, deploy your project to a production environment (like Vercel or Railway) and link your live dashboard to your GitHub README. This proves your app is a living, breathing product.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the grade and start building for the career. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap. The only thing missing is your first commit. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
